title: Theory of Computation (ToC) | True/False Quiz
heading: ToC Fundamentals: True/False Challenge
description: Test your knowledge on regular languages, context-free grammars, DFA, NDFA, pumping lemma, and closure properties with True/False questions only.

questions.question: The closure properties of regular languages include union, concatenation, and complement.
questions.explanation: True: Regular languages are closed under union, concatenation, Kleene star, complement, intersection, and many other operations.

questions.question: Ambiguity in a context-free grammar occurs when a string has more than one leftmost derivation.
questions.explanation: True: A CFG is ambiguous if there exists at least one string that can be derived by two different leftmost derivations (or parse trees).

questions.question: The Pumping Lemma for regular languages can be used to prove that a language is regular.
questions.explanation: False: The pumping lemma is used to prove that a language is NOT regular. It cannot prove regularity.

questions.question: Simplification of a context-free grammar can include removing useless symbols and productions.
questions.explanation: True: CFG simplification involves removing useless symbols (non-generating and unreachable), ε-productions, and unit productions.

questions.question: Every NDFA has an equivalent DFA.
questions.explanation: True: Nondeterministic Finite Automata can be converted to equivalent Deterministic Finite Automata using subset construction.

questions.question: DFA can have ε-transitions.
questions.explanation: False: DFA does not allow ε-transitions. All transitions in a DFA are on input symbols only.

questions.question: NDFA can have ε-transitions.
questions.explanation: True: NDFA (or NFA) can have ε-transitions, allowing state changes without consuming input symbols.

questions.question: Regular expressions can represent all context-free languages.
questions.explanation: False: Regular expressions represent only regular languages, which are a proper subset of context-free languages.

questions.question: A deterministic finite automaton can have multiple transitions for the same input symbol.
questions.explanation: False: DFA has exactly one transition per input symbol from each state. Multiple transitions are not allowed.

questions.question: A parse tree represents the syntactic structure of a string.
questions.explanation: True: A parse tree (or derivation tree) shows how a string is derived from the start symbol using grammar productions.

questions.question: All regular languages are also context-free languages.
questions.explanation: True: Regular languages form a proper subset of context-free languages. Every regular language is context-free.

questions.question: Context-free languages are closed under intersection.
questions.explanation: False: CFLs are not closed under intersection. However, they are closed under union, concatenation, and Kleene star.

questions.question: The Kleene Star operation allows zero or more repetitions of a symbol.
questions.explanation: True: Kleene star (*) applied to a language L yields L* = {ε} ∪ L ∪ LL ∪ LLL ∪ ... (zero or more repetitions).

questions.question: A grammar is ambiguous if it produces no strings.
questions.explanation: False: Ambiguity refers to multiple derivations for some string. A grammar producing no strings is empty, not ambiguous.

questions.question: Minimization of DFA reduces the number of states without changing the language.
questions.explanation: True: DFA minimization (using Hopcroft's or Moore's algorithm) produces an equivalent DFA with the minimal number of states.

questions.question: The Pumping Lemma for CFL is used to prove that a language is context-free.
questions.explanation: False: Like the regular version, the pumping lemma for CFL proves that a language is NOT context-free, not that it is context-free.

questions.question: A terminal symbol can be replaced by other symbols in a CFG.
questions.explanation: False: Terminal symbols appear in the generated strings and cannot be replaced. Only non-terminals can be replaced using productions.

questions.question: A non-terminal symbol can be replaced using production rules.
questions.explanation: True: Production rules define how non-terminals can be replaced by strings of terminals and/or non-terminals.

questions.question: A Pushdown Automaton is more powerful than a Finite Automaton.
questions.explanation: True: PDA (with stack) recognizes context-free languages, while FA recognizes regular languages. CFL ⊃ Regular languages.

questions.question: Every context-free grammar is unambiguous.
questions.explanation: False: Many context-free grammars are ambiguous. Some CFLs are inherently ambiguous (every grammar for them is ambiguous).
